title: phyteuma corniculatum subsp. charmelioides
intro: Phyteuma corniculatum subsp. charmelioides, also known as Alpine Phyteuma or Alpine Rampion Bellflower, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the bellflower family (Campanulaceae). This plant is used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Phyteuma corniculatum subsp. charmelioides include reducing inflammation and improving respiratory health, as well as potentially having a protective effect on the cardiovascular system. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made into teas, tinctures, and infusions, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety for human consumption.
